paper.title: Excitation: Momentum For Experts
paper.abstract: We propose Excitation, a novel optimization framework designed to accelerate learning in sparse architectures such as Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs). Unlike traditional optimizers that treat all parameters uniformly, Excitation dynamically modulates updates using batch-level expert utilization. It introduces a competitive update dynamic that amplifies updates to highly-utilized experts and can selectively suppress low-utilization ones, effectively sharpening routing specialization. Notably, we identi

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: Excitation reduces memory usage by 20-30% in sparse architectures

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Excitation's competitive update dynamic can selectively suppress low-utilization experts, effectively sharpening routing specialization.
technical_details: No additional per-parameter optimizer state or learnable parameters
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, Excitation can be deployed on both mobile and laptop platforms due to its optimizer-agnostic nature and minimal integration effort.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
